GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "This hybrid sleep mask is missing from your skincare routine", "It's new, from an Australian brand, and delivers glowy skin come sunrise.", "We're calling it: the sleep mask is 2020's beauty MVP. Hydrating, nourishing and reparative, overnight masks - the ones that sink in while you sleep, no rinsing necessary - are the hands-off approach to the kind of glowing complexion you would expect from brawnier in-clinic treatments. It's also why CEMOY (pronounced 'see more') — a relative newcomer to the skincare market — have launched their own riff on the trusty skin hero. CEMOY's interpretation is a hybrid of two of your favourite products: a night cream and a sleep mask. The result? Inception Sleeping Cream and Mask: a velvety rich cream - not unlike a stand- alone night cream — but with the added 'slip' of a mask. On application, it floats on the surface before absorbing into the epidermis, delivering slow-release hydration when the skin is naturally in repair mode. It's a smart move. Optimising this overnight recovery window is important, and the dual benefits of a mask and cream mean it works double duty, too. In the same way that our muscles recuperate and build strength when we rest after a workout, vital repair processes take place in our skin in the evening when it's not busy fighting off external aggressors like UV rays and pollution. This is the time when collagen production (that wonderful plumping agent) goes into overdrive, and we replace old skin cells with new ones. These processes happen naturally in our youth, but they get lazier as early as our twenties, showing up as fine lines, dehydration and an overall lacklustre complexion. For this reason, the Inception Sleeping Cream and Mask not only boosts skin recovery, it also replenishes moisture and collagen stores via a host of results-driven ingredients. Orange roughy oil delivers intense hydration while antioxidant-rich hibiscus flowers and baobab fruit maintain elastin (responsible for firmness) in the skin. It's also packed with squalene — among the buzziest ingredients in skincare right now — which strengthens the skin's vital protective barrier as well as trusty skincare stalwarts like hyaluronic acid (more hydration) and vitamin E (more antioxidants). While an efficient formula is key for CEMOY, the notion that skincare and self-care go hand-in-hand is tantamount. Channel your inner facialist with the accompanying ergonomic non-slip brush which issues an even layer of product from forehead to neck. The packaging too, is equally thoughtful with its weighty, gold and navy vessel worthy of being left on full-display in your bathroom. Moreover, as well as doubling as a night cream and sleep mask, the formula also boasts the versatility to be worn as a typical brightening mask: apply an even layer to the skin and wash off after 15 minutes for a quick beauty boost. Or simply do as we do and pop it on before drifting into a restful slumber."
